citation_and_locator: Lee, Peppina Po-lun. 2019. Focus Manifestation in Mandarin Chinese and Cantonese: A Comparative Perspective. Routledge; preview introduction pp. 4–7 and contents pp. vi–viii
what_it_supports: Explicitly recognizes Cantonese `lin4 … dou1` as a syntactic/grammatical focus construction; identifies Cantonese `dou1` as `even/also`; places particle-associate scope in a linear focus system.
limit: Preview does not expose the complete analysis, full judgment set, polarity matrix, or ordinary-XP `lin4` omission conditions.

citation_and_locator: Lau, Cindy Wan Yee. 2020. Two uses of dou in Cantonese. Linguistic Society of Hong Kong Annual Research Forum abstract book, p. 28
what_it_supports: Distinguishes additive and scalar uses of `dou`; scalar use conveys unexpectedness and may co-occur with `(samzi)lin`; gives an extreme least-likely focused alternative.
limit: Conference abstract only; analytical `dou1`/`dou2` subscripts do not establish a lexical tone split, complete syntax, or population-wide judgments.

citation_and_locator: Chinese University of Hong Kong, Independent Learning Centre. Cantonese Express, Daily Conversation 3, comparison item 5 `連……都 lin4……dou1`
what_it_supports: Overt construction in affirmative `連星期六都要上堂` and negative `連龍肉都食唔落` examples, with nominal associates.
limit: Pedagogical examples do not establish exhaustiveness, optionality, fine-grained acceptability, or formal semantics.

citation_and_locator: Lee, Tsoi Lam Sharon. 2019. Focus prosody in Cantonese: the case of verb-copying constructions. MA thesis, University of Hong Kong, DOI 10.5353/th_991044166179403414; structural discussion and HKU record
what_it_supports: Shows that the distinct Cantonese verb-copy focus family permits and prefers absent `lin4`, while requiring verb copying and a licensor; provides a 10-speaker prosody experiment.
limit: The omission claim concerns `V1-dou1-licensor-V2`, not ordinary `lin4 XP dou1`; it cannot license global optionality.

citation_and_locator: Law, Yan Kei Ann. 2004. Sentence-final focus particles in Cantonese. PhD dissertation, University College London; chapter 5
what_it_supports: Cantonese additive/scalar focus context and interactions involving `dou1` and sentence-final particles.
limit: Does not directly establish ordinary `lin4 XP dou1` form or `lin4` optionality.
